clusters.title: US Responds to Iran's Alleged Downing of Helicopter
clusters.summary: President Trump has accused Iran of shooting down a US Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz, prompting a vow for retaliation. The US Central Command confirmed that military actions have been initiated in response to the incident, which heightens tensions in the region. This escalation underscores ongoing conflicts between the two nations and raises concerns about further military confrontations.

clusters.title: House Approves $70 Billion Immigration Enforcement Bill
clusters.summary: The House of Representatives has passed a $70 billion bill aimed at funding immigration enforcement agencies, including ICE and Border Patrol, through the end of President Donald Trump's term. The legislation faced several challenges before securing a narrow approval, reflecting ongoing partisan divisions over immigration policy. This funding is significant as it will shape enforcement priorities and operations during the final months of the Trump administration.

items.description: The vote gave final passage to Republicans’ megabill to fund immigration enforcement through the remainder of President Trump’s term, clearing it for his signature.

items.description: After weeks of setbacks and delays, Republicans narrowly passed a roughly $70 billion package to fund ICE and Border Patrol through the end of President Donald Trump’s term.

clusters.title: NASA Names Artemis III Crew for Upcoming Moon Mission
clusters.summary: NASA has officially announced the crew for the Artemis III mission, which is designed to test essential systems for future lunar exploration. This mission is a critical step towards the planned Artemis V Moon landing scheduled for 2028. The selected astronauts will play a key role in advancing the agency's goals for sustainable lunar presence and exploration.

items.title: NASA names Artemis III crew for high-stakes lander test - USA Today
items.description: NASA names Artemis III crew for high-stakes lander test  USA Today

items.title: Nasa has named the Artemis III crew - what is their mission?
items.description: Artemis III will help test crucial systems needed for the planned Artemis V Moon landing in 2028.

items.title: ‘Earth’s first starfleet’: Nasa reveals Artemis III crew and project’s next steps
items.description:
Luca Parmitano to pilot all-male crew of four paving way for planned first human landing on Artemis IV in 2028

Jared Isaacman, the Nasa administrator, hailed the creation of “Earth’s first starfleet” on Tuesday as he revealed the Artemis III crew and details of the next stages of the space agency’s project to return humans to the moon.

An Italian astronaut, Luca Parmitano of the European Space Agency (ESA), will be the pilot of the planned two-week mission to lower Earth orbit next year that will test lunar landers from private companies Blue Origin and SpaceX.

Continue reading...

items.title: NASA reveals the new Artemis III crew
items.description: NASA has announced the new crew of astronauts that will take part in the Artemis III mission to test new moon landers. Randy Bresnik, Luca Parmitano, Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas are scheduled to launch into Earth orbit next year to test out at least one of the commercially developed lunar landers expected to carry NASA astronauts to the surface of the moon in 2028.

image.title: NASA’s Orion Spacecraft Completes Milestone for Artemis III Crew Module
image.author: NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center

clusters.title: Karmelo Anthony Convicted of Murder in Frisco Track Meet Stabbing
clusters.summary: Karmelo Anthony, 19, was found guilty of murder in the fatal stabbing of 17-year-old Austin Metcalf during a district-wide track meet in Frisco, Texas. The incident occurred in 2025, and Anthony's defense claimed he acted in self-defense amid a confrontation. The jury's verdict highlights the ongoing concerns surrounding youth violence and safety at school events.

items.title: Jury reaches guilty verdict in Karmelo Anthony murder trial
items.description: Karmelo Anthony, 19, was found guilty of the 2025 murder of fellow high school student Austin Metcalf at a district-wide meet in Frisco, a Dallas suburb. Anthony’s legal team argued he acted in self-defense after he’d sat in the bleachers under the tent of rival high school Memorial and was confronted by members of its track team and told to leave.

clusters.title: 7.8 Magnitude Earthquake in the Philippines Displaces Thousands
clusters.summary: A powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck the Philippines, resulting in at least 37 fatalities and displacing approximately 32,000 individuals. The quake caused significant destruction, including the collapse of buildings and triggered a tsunami warning in affected areas. Emergency response efforts are underway to assist those impacted by the disaster.

clusters.summary: Decarlos Brown Jr. has been deemed incompetent to stand trial for the fatal stabbing of Iryna Zarutska, a Ukrainian refugee, on a train in North Carolina. This case highlights ongoing issues surrounding mental health evaluations in the criminal justice system.

clusters.title: Former Air Canada Pilot Charged for Flying Without License
clusters.summary: Geoffrey Wall, a former Air Canada pilot, has been charged by Ontario police for allegedly operating over 900 flights without the required pilot's license from 2009 to 2025. The case raises significant concerns about aviation safety and regulatory compliance in the airline industry. Authorities are investigating the implications of his unauthorized flights on passengers and air travel standards.

items.title: Former Air Canada pilot flew hundreds of flights without proper license, police say
items.description: A former pilot flew commercial planes for nearly 17 years allegedly without the required license, Ontario police said

items.title: Former Air Canada pilot charged after allegedly flying without proper license for 16 years
items.description:
Geoffrey Wall is alleged to have flown over 900 flights domestically and internationally between 2009 and 2025

A former Air Canada pilot has been charged after flying for years without a proper license, Canadian police have said.

Geoffrey Wall, of Barrie, Ontario, is alleged to have operated as an airline captain between 2009 and 2025 without a license to fly large commercial passenger planes, according to Peel regional police.

Continue reading...
